title: [lichen_l2.c:702] lichen_peer_remove no check for UNINIT state
description:
Contract: lichen_peer_remove should reject operations when module is not initialized.
Violation: Unlike lichen_peer_add() which checks lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() == NULL (line 641) to detect UNINIT state, lichen_peer_remove() only checks needs_reinit() (line 718) but not UNINIT. A call to peer_remove() before init() would acquire rx_mutex and search peer_table, which is harmless but inconsistent with peer_add() contract.
Impact: Low - the operation would return -ENOENT since peer_table is empty/uninitialized before init. However, contract inconsistency between sibling functions is a code smell.
Mitigation: Add the same UNINIT check as peer_add() for contract symmetry.
close_reason: Added UNINIT state check to lichen_peer_remove() using the same pattern as lichen_peer_add(): checks lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() == NULL before acquiring mutex, returns -ENODEV with error log if module not initialized
